label: Directory sync fixtures
description: Watches a stuffed local folder and reconciles adds, updates, and deletions back into the indexed document set on a scheduler.

label: URL sync fixture
description: Fetches a local AbsoluteJS route and reindexes it through the same sync contract used by the core RAG plugin.

label: Site discovery fixture
description: Discovers local site pages through robots, sitemap, canonical, nofollow, and noindex fixture routes so the ops surface can show discovery diagnostics on the same sync cards as every other source.

label: Storage sync fixture
description: Simulates Bun-native S3 object sync on the same ops surface used for directories and URLs, so storage-backed knowledge bases stay first-class in the demo.

metadata.liveReady: Ready for a real S3 or R2 bucket through Bun's native S3 client.

label: Gmail sync fixture
description: Shows the Gmail adapter path with a support mailbox thread and attachment lineage, so switching to a real OAuth-backed Gmail account is a straight adapter swap instead of a different workflow.

metadata.liveReady: Ready for a real Gmail OAuth token and mailbox query.

label: Graph sync fixture
description: Shows the Microsoft Graph adapter path with thread-aware mailbox sync and attachment lineage, so enterprise Outlook/365 mail can use the same AbsoluteJS sync surface.

metadata.liveReady: Ready for a real Microsoft Graph OAuth token and mailbox query.

label: IMAP sync fixture
description: Shows the protocol-level IMAP adapter path, so a generic mailbox can sync through the same AbsoluteJS email workflow without being locked to one hosted provider.

metadata.liveReady: Ready for a real IMAP host, mailbox, and credentials.
